description: A person can 'rato' towards a voice, and a wheel can 'rato' on its axle. The verb changes facing or direction, or rotates a body or object. For a colour or condition becoming different, 'kelu' (become) carries the change of state.
articulatory_notes: Tap or briefly trill the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for 'r' and open into stressed central 'a'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't', release it, and round the lips for middle-height back 'o'.
sound_symbolism: The tongue begins with a small roll, then moves forward to a dental stop before the lips round at the end. That change of contact gives the word an audible turn of its own.
usage_notes: The verb is intransitive when the subject changes its own facing, and transitive when the subject turns an object. An intended direction takes 'wea' (TOWARD).

semantic_domains.activity: redirecting oneself or turning another object
semantic_domains.physical: rotation around a point or axis
semantic_domains.spatial: a change of facing or direction
